heading: Management of buildings by Administrator of General Services

children.heading: .—Request by Federal Agency or Instrumentality
children.label: a .—Request by Federal Agency or Instrumentality
children.text: At the request of a federal agency, a mixed-ownership Government corporation (as defined in chapter 91 of title 31), or the District of Columbia, the Administrator of General Services may operate, maintain, and protect a building that is owned by the Federal Government (or, in the case of a wholly owned or mixed-ownership Government corporation, by the corporation) and occupied by the agency or instrumentality making the request.

children.heading: Transfer of Functions by Director of the Office of Management and Budget.—
children.label: b Transfer of Functions by Director of the Office of Management and Budget.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: When the Director of the Office of Management and Budget determines that it is in the interest of economy or efficiency, the Director shall transfer to the Administrator all functions vested in a federal agency with respect to the operation, maintenance, and custody of an office building owned by the Government or a wholly owned Government corporation, or an office building, or part of an office building, that is occupied by a federal agency under a lease.

children.heading: .—Exception for post-office buildings
children.label: 2 .—Exception for post-office buildings
children.text: A transfer of functions shall not be made under this subsection for a post-office building, unless the Director determines that the building is not used predominantly for post-office purposes. The Administrator may delegate functions with respect to a post-office building that are transferred to the Administrator under this subsection only to another officer or employee of the General Services Administration or to the Postmaster General.

children.heading: .—Exception for buildings in a foreign country
children.label: 3 .—Exception for buildings in a foreign country
children.text: A transfer of functions shall not be made under this subsection for a building located in a foreign country.

children.heading: .—Exception for department of defense buildings
children.label: 4 .—Exception for department of defense buildings
children.text: A transfer of functions shall not be made under this subsection for a building located on the grounds of a facility of the Department of Defense (including a fort, camp, post, arsenal, navy yard, naval training station, airfield, proving ground, military supply depot, or school) unless and only to the extent that the Secretary of Defense has issued a permit for use by another agency.

children.heading: .—Exception for groups of special purpose buildings
children.label: 5 .—Exception for groups of special purpose buildings
B
children.text: used wholly or predominantly for the special purposes of the agency with custody of the buildings; and

children.label: C
children.text: not generally suitable for use by another agency.

children.heading: .—Exception for certain government buildings
children.label: 6 .—Exception for certain government buildings
children.text: A transfer of functions shall not be made under this subsection for the Treasury Building, the Bureau of Engraving and Printing Building, the buildings occupied by the National Institute of Standards and Technology, and the buildings under the jurisdiction of the regents of the Smithsonian Institution.
